config_yaml_params.note: EMA(50) in config is NOT used — session_runner hardcodes EMA(20)

signal_logic.long_entry: rsi[i-1] < 35 AND rsi[i] >= 35 AND close[i] > ema[i]
signal_logic.short_entry: rsi[i-1] > 65 AND rsi[i] <= 65 AND close[i] < ema[i]
signal_logic.scan_mode: batch - iterates all bars each poll cycle

scanner_bar_source.note: Only today's bars used for intraday signals; bar count grows by 1 every 5 minutes

today_snow_actual.reason: Session ended with 22 bars, 3 short of required 25

rsi_signal_that_existed_but_blocked.condition: rsi crossed up from oversold AND price above EMA

2026-05-25.note: No symbols confirmed, no insufficient_bars warnings for any strategy in trading context

2026-05-23.note: Weekend session, no symbols confirmed, no strategy warnings

2026-05-12.note: Session started 9:36 ET with fallback to manual symbols, trades placed via fallback. RSI_trend not monitored in logs for that session independently.

H1.hypothesis: RSI_trend becomes eligible after the highest-quality momentum window has already passed.
evidence: Day high occurred at 10:00 ET, 90 minutes before structural readiness (11:30 ET)
Day low occurred at 09:35 ET, 115 minutes before structural readiness
100% of total intraday range consumed before 11:30 ET
Post-readiness range available: 2.37 vs pre-readiness range: 6.72
This is a structural constraint, not incidental — applies every session

H2.hypothesis: Options spread deterioration increases materially before RSI_trend entries.
evidence: SNOW spread was 8.18% at 10:33 ET (near system max of 10%)
SNOW spread improved to 3.67% by 11:04 ET
Single data point — insufficient for systematic claim
Directionally: if RSI_trend were ready at 10:33, spread cost would have been higher

H3.hypothesis: RSI_trend is more suitable for trend continuation / midday continuation than opening momentum.
evidence: Two-hour structural warmup excludes opening momentum by design
RSI(14) oversold/overbought crossover logic detects exhaustion/bounce, not breakouts
EMA trend filter requires established trend — not useful at open before trend forms
Post-readiness SNOW RSI was 36-48, never crossed 35/65 thresholds — no signal even when ready
Strategy architecture is textbook mean-reversion/continuation, not momentum-entry

H4.hypothesis: RSI_trend may perform better as a confirmation filter rather than primary trigger.
evidence: RSI + EMA is commonly used as secondary confirmation in multi-factor systems
Today's RSI values (36-48) were aligned with scanner LONG signal — consistent with filter role
No backtest data available to quantify filter vs primary trigger comparison
Would require architectural change: current logic requires RSI threshold crossing, not just trend alignment

structural_latency_sources.name: Minimum bars requirement
structural_latency_sources.description: min_rows = max(rsi_period, trend_ema_period) + 5 = 25 at deployed settings
structural_latency_sources.impact: 120-minute warmup at 5-min bars from first bar; strategy produces no signals before this

structural_latency_sources.name: Batch re-evaluation each poll cycle
structural_latency_sources.description: generate_signals() iterates range(1, len(bars)) on every 60s poll — no incremental state
structural_latency_sources.impact: Binary gate — 0 signals until bar 25, no partial readiness

structural_latency_sources.name: 5-minute bar interval
structural_latency_sources.description: Scanner uses interval='5m' for all intraday data; bar count increments by 1 every 5 minutes
structural_latency_sources.impact: Multiplies bar count delay by 5x vs 1-min resolution; makes RSI_trend ready 2h after open

structural_latency_sources.name: EWM RSI initialization noise
structural_latency_sources.description: RSI uses EWM com=13 which requires many bars to converge; +5 buffer compensates partially
structural_latency_sources.impact: Minor — early RSI values are less reliable but this is mitigated by the bar count gate

structural_latency_sources.name: Session start delay
structural_latency_sources.description: Today's session started at 10:03 ET instead of 9:30 ET
structural_latency_sources.impact: Incidental — did not change structural minimum (11:30 ET). Did prevent early bar accumulation.

structural_latency_sources.name: Symbol confirmation delay
structural_latency_sources.description: SNOW not confirmed until 10:33 ET (63 min after open, 30 min after session start)
structural_latency_sources.impact: Incidental — pushed actual readiness from structural 11:30 to operational 11:33; session ended at 11:19

structural_latency_sources.name: Config vs runtime EMA period discrepancy
structural_latency_sources.description: config.yaml specifies trend_ema_period=50 (min_rows=55), session_runner.py hardcodes trend_ema_period=20 (min_rows=25)
structural_latency_sources.impact: EMA(50) config has no effect. If intended, it would require 55 bars = ~270 min delay = strategy never fires intraday.

observations: RSI_trend has not produced a single signal across 5 observed sessions (2026-05-11 through 2026-05-26). Combined causes: (1) bar warmup rarely completed, (2) when bars are sufficient, RSI does not cross the 35/65 thresholds.
The only session where trades occurred (2026-05-12) used a fallback symbol list — RSI_trend was not the trigger; the scan logic defaulted to manual watchlist without RSI strategy evaluation.

Today's SNOW RSI dipped to 33.0 at 10:36 ET — below the 35 oversold threshold — and was trending up. This is exactly the LONG entry condition RSI_trend is designed to catch. It was blocked purely by the bar count (14 < 25).
ORB reached its bar threshold (~10:50 ET) but also produced 0 signals today. The opening-range high may have already been set before the ORB range period was complete.

At 5-min resolution, the order of readiness (VWAP < ORB < RSI_trend) is inversely correlated with the quality of the momentum opportunity: VWAP can act early, RSI_trend acts last when momentum is exhausted.

The scanner uses daily RSI (from daily_df) for scoring, which showed SNOW RSI=80 — flagged as extreme but still passed with score=40 (ATR + trend_up). The daily RSI=80 (overbought) is at odds with the intraday RSI=33-48 seen at the bar level. These are different time frames and both valid.

SNOW was the only symbol to pass the scanner on 2026-05-26. All other 26 symbols were rejected for low_volume_chop (rvol < 0.5). The top-scored symbols (MSFT, GOOGL, AVGO, NET at score=70) all failed the rvol filter.
